 {"id":"brenner_bot-r4rf","title":"Task: Write Research Program Unit Tests","description":"# Task: Write Research Program Unit Tests

## Objective
Comprehensive tests for program schema, storage, metrics, and CLI.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/research-program.test.ts`

### Test Coverage

#### Schema Tests
```typescript
describe('ResearchProgramSchema', () => {
  it('accepts valid program', () => { ... });
  it('requires minimum description length', () => { ... });
  it('validates ID format', () => { ... });
  it('accepts optional tags and notes', () => { ... });
});
```

#### Storage Tests
```typescript
describe('Program Storage', () => {
  it('creates program with generated ID', () => { ... });
  it('loads program by ID', () => { ... });
  it('updates program metadata', () => { ... });
  it('adds session to program', () => { ... });
  it('removes session from program', () => { ... });
  it('preserves session order', () => { ... });
  it('lists programs by status', () => { ... });
  it('finds program for session', () => { ... });
});
```

#### Metrics Tests
```typescript
describe('Program Metrics', () => {
  it('computes hypothesis funnel correctly', () => { ... });
  it('calculates kill rate', () => { ... });
  it('computes test execution rate', () => { ... });
  it('calculates average session score', () => { ... });
  it('determines trend from chronological scores', () => { ... });
  it('computes days active', () => { ... });
});

describe('Program Health', () => {
  it('returns healthy for active program with good metrics', () => { ... });
  it('returns stalled for low kill rate', () => { ... });
  it('returns blocked for many blocked tests', () => { ... });
});
```

#### CLI Tests
```typescript
describe('Program CLI', () => {
  it('creates program with all required fields', () => { ... });
  it('adds session to program', () => { ... });
  it('lists programs in table format', () => { ... });
  it('shows dashboard with all sections', () => { ... });
  it('exports to markdown format', () => { ... });
});
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema validation tested
- [ ] Storage operations tested
- [ ] Metrics computation verified
- [ ] Health assessment logic tested
- [ ] CLI commands tested
- [ ] Edge cases covered (empty programs, single session, etc.)

## Dependencies
- All other Research Program Dashboard tasks

## Effort Estimate

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew","title":"EPIC: Session Experience & UI - Orchestration, State Machines, Progress","description":"# Session Experience & UI - Orchestration, State Machines, Progress

## Purpose
Design and implement the user experience for running complete Brenner Loop sessions, from hypothesis intake through final research brief.

## The Session Flow

### Phase Progression
```
intake → sharpening → level_split → exclusion_test → 
object_transpose → scale_check → agent_dispatch → 
synthesis → evidence_gathering → revision → complete
```

Not all phases are required. User can:
- Skip operators that don't apply
- Jump directly to synthesis if operators completed
- Return to earlier phases to revise

### State Machine
```typescript
type SessionPhase =
  | 'intake'           // Initial hypothesis entry
  | 'sharpening'       // Socratic refinement
  | 'level_split'      // Σ operator
  | 'exclusion_test'   // ⊘ operator
  | 'object_transpose' // ⟳ operator
  | 'scale_check'      // ⊙ operator
  | 'agent_dispatch'   // Send to tribunal
  | 'synthesis'        // Aggregate responses
  | 'evidence_gathering' // Record tests & results
  | 'revision'         // Update hypothesis
  | 'complete';        // Generate final brief

interface SessionState {
  phase: SessionPhase;
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard;
  operatorResults: Partial<Record<OperatorType, OperatorResult>>;
  agentResponses: AgentResponse[];
  evidenceEntries: EvidenceEntry[];
  artifacts: GeneratedArtifact[];
  
  // Navigation
  canGoBack: boolean;
  canGoForward: boolean;
  availablePhases: SessionPhase[];
}
```

## UI Components

### Session Dashboard
Main view showing:
- Current phase (highlighted in timeline)
- Hypothesis card (always visible, editable in some phases)
- Phase-specific content area
- Progress indicators
- Quick actions

### Phase Timeline
Horizontal or vertical timeline showing:
- Completed phases (checkmark)
- Current phase (highlighted)
- Available phases (active)
- Skipped phases (dimmed)
- Locked phases (grayed out)

### Hypothesis Card Component
Always-visible card showing:
- Current statement
- Confidence score
- Version number
- Quick edit capability
- Evolution history popup

### Operator Panel
For each operator:
- Guided step-by-step UI
- Generated content display
- User selection/input areas
- Brenner quote sidebar

### Agent Tribunal Panel
Shows:
- Agent status (pending/running/complete)
- Response previews
- Expandable full responses
- Conflict/agreement indicators

### Evidence Ledger Panel
Displays:
- Timeline of tests
- Add new evidence button
- Confidence chart
- Robustness assessment

## Mobile Considerations
Session experience should work on tablet/mobile:
- Phase timeline becomes vertical
- Cards stack vertically
- Agent responses in accordion
- Full functionality preserved

## Keyboard Navigation
Power users should be able to:
- Navigate phases with arrow keys
- Trigger actions with shortcuts
- Jump to specific phases

## Persistence
Sessions auto-save:
- Every state change persisted
- Resume from where you left off
- No explicit save button needed

## Dependencies
- All other epics (orchestrates them all)

## Success Criteria
- Users can complete full sessions smoothly
- Progress is always clear
- Navigation is intuitive
- Mobile experience is usable
- Sessions survive browser refresh

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.1","title":"Design Session Dashboard Layout","description":"# Design Session Dashboard Layout

## Purpose
Create the main session dashboard layout that users see during active sessions.

## Layout Design
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  BrennerBot Lab                               [Session: SM-001] │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ [Intake] → [Split] → [Exclude] → [Transpose] → [Scale] │   │
│  │    ●         ●         ◐            ○            ○      │   │
│  │ complete  complete  current      locked       locked    │   │
│  │                                                         │   │
│  │ → [Agents] → [Evidence] → [Synthesis] → [Brief]        │   │
│  │      ○           ○            ○           ○             │   │
│  │   locked      locked       locked      locked           │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────┐ ┌───────────────────────┐   │
│  │ CURRENT PHASE                 │ │ HYPOTHESIS CARD       │   │
│  │ ══════════════                │ │ ═══════════════       │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │ Exclusion Test (⊘)            │ │ H₂ (v3)      47%     │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │ "What would prove you wrong?" │ │ "Algorithm-driven..." │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │ [Phase-specific content]      │ │ [Edit] [History]      │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │                               │ └───────────────────────┘   │
│  │                               │                             │
│  │                               │ ┌───────────────────────┐   │
│  │                               │ │ 💬 BRENNER SAYS       │   │
│  │                               │ │                       │   │
│  │                               │ │ "Not merely unlikely  │   │
│  │                               │ │  — impossible..."     │   │
│  │                               │ │          — §89        │   │
│  └───────────────────────────────┘ └───────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  [← Previous]                                      [Next →]     │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Key Components
1. **Phase Timeline**: Shows progress through session
2. **Phase Content Area**: Main interactive area
3. **Hypothesis Card**: Always-visible summary
4. **Brenner Sidebar**: Contextual quotes
5. **Navigation**: Previous/Next controls

## Responsive Behavior
- **Desktop**: Side-by-side layout as shown
- **Tablet**: Stacked layout, hypothesis card collapsible
- **Mobile**: Single column, cards in accordion

## File Location
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/SessionDashboard.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Layout clear and intuitive
- [ ] All key information visible
- [ ] Responsive across devices
- [ ] Phase switching is smooth

-{"id":"brenner_bot-reew.2","title":"Build Phase Timeline Component","description":"# Build Phase Timeline Component

## Purpose
Create the visual timeline showing session phases and progress.

## Phase Flow
```
intake → sharpening → level_split → exclusion_test → 
object_transpose → scale_check → agent_dispatch → 
synthesis → evidence_gathering → revision → complete
```

## Visual States
- **Complete**: Filled circle (●), green
- **Current**: Half-filled (◐), blue, highlighted
- **Available**: Empty circle (○), gray, clickable
- **Locked**: Dimmed circle, no interaction
- **Skipped**: Strikethrough, dimmed

## Component API
```typescript
interface PhaseTimelineProps {
  phases: SessionPhase[];
  currentPhase: SessionPhase;
  completedPhases: SessionPhase[];
  availablePhases: SessionPhase[];
  skippedPhases: SessionPhase[];
  onPhaseClick: (phase: SessionPhase) => void;
}
```

## Responsive Behavior
- **Desktop**: Horizontal timeline with labels
- **Tablet**: Horizontal timeline, abbreviated labels
- **Mobile**: Vertical timeline or stepper

## Keyboard Navigation
- Arrow keys to navigate phases
- Enter to select phase
- Visual focus indicator

## Animation
- Smooth transition between phases
- Completion animation (checkmark appear)
- Progress indicator animation

## File Location
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/PhaseTimeline.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All phase states displayed correctly
- [ ] Phase switching works
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Responsive layout works
- [ ] Animations are smooth

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.3","title":"Implement Session State Machine","description":"# Implement Session State Machine

## Purpose
Create a formal state machine that governs session phase transitions.

## Why a State Machine
Session progression has complex rules:
- Some phases depend on others
- Some phases can be skipped
- Some phases can be revisited
- Invalid transitions should be impossible

A state machine makes this explicit and prevents bugs.

## State Definition
```typescript
const sessionMachine = createMachine({
  id: 'session',
  initial: 'intake',
  
  states: {
    intake: {
      on: { SUBMIT_HYPOTHESIS: 'sharpening' }
    },
    
    sharpening: {
      on: {
        REFINE: 'sharpening',  // Stay and refine more
        CONTINUE: 'level_split',
        SKIP_OPERATORS: 'agent_dispatch'
      }
    },
    
    level_split: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'exclusion_test',
        SKIP: 'exclusion_test',
        BACK: 'sharpening'
      }
    },
    
    exclusion_test: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'object_transpose',
        SKIP: 'object_transpose',
        BACK: 'level_split'
      }
    },
    
    object_transpose: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'scale_check',
        SKIP: 'scale_check',
        BACK: 'exclusion_test'
      }
    },
    
    scale_check: {
      on: {
        COMPLETE: 'agent_dispatch',
        SKIP: 'agent_dispatch',
        BACK: 'object_transpose'
      }
    },
    
    agent_dispatch: {
      on: {
        RESPONSES_RECEIVED: 'synthesis',
        SKIP_AGENTS: 'evidence_gathering',
        BACK: 'scale_check'
      }
    },
    
    synthesis: {
      on: {
        CONTINUE: 'evidence_gathering',
        BACK: 'agent_dispatch'
      }
    },
    
    evidence_gathering: {
      on: {
        ADD_EVIDENCE: 'evidence_gathering',
        REVISE_HYPOTHESIS: 'revision',
        COMPLETE: 'complete',
        BACK: 'synthesis'
      }
    },
    
    revision: {
      on: {
        SAVE: 'evidence_gathering',
        RESTART_OPERATORS: 'level_split'
      }
    },
    
    complete: {
      type: 'final'
    }
  }
});
```

## Integration
Use XState or similar, or custom implementation:
```typescript
function useSessionMachine(initialState: SessionPhase) {
  // Returns current state, available transitions, send function
}
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/session-machine.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All states and transitions defined
- [ ] Invalid transitions impossible
- [ ] State persists with session
- [ ] Hook provides clean API

Includes: all 11 phases as states, SessionEvent types, transition guards (hasPredictions, hasPrimaryHypothesis, etc.), transition() function, useSessionMachine hook with React integration, convenience actions (advance, goBack, goToPhase), phase display helpers.  {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.4","title":"Create Session List & Management Page","description":"# Create Session List & Management Page

## Purpose
Build the page where users see all their sessions and can create new ones.

## Page Layout
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  BrennerBot Lab                           [+ New Session]       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Your Research Sessions                                         │
│  ═══════════════════════                                        │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ SM-2024-003                              2 hours ago    │   │
│  │ "Algorithm-driven content causes teen depression"       │   │
│  │ Phase: Evidence Gathering │ Confidence: 37% │ v3        │   │
│  │ [Continue] [Export] [Delete]                            │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ SM-2024-002                              1 day ago      │   │
│  │ "Meditation reduces anxiety through attention training"  │   │
│  │ Phase: Complete │ Confidence: 72% │ v2                  │   │
│  │ [View Brief] [Export] [Delete]                          │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ SM-2024-001                              3 days ago     │   │
│  │ "Remote work improves productivity"                     │   │
│  │ Phase: Exclusion Test │ Confidence: 65% │ v1            │   │
│  │ [Continue] [Export] [Delete]                            │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
│  [Load More]                                                    │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Features

### Session Card
Shows:
- Session ID
- Hypothesis preview
- Current phase
- Confidence score
- Last updated
- Quick actions

### Filtering & Sorting
- Sort by: Date, Confidence, Phase
- Filter by: Status (active, complete), Confidence range

### Actions
- **Continue**: Resume in-progress session
- **View Brief**: For complete sessions
- **Export**: Download as Markdown/PDF/JSON
- **Delete**: With confirmation

### New Session Flow
Click "+ New Session" → Hypothesis Intake form

## Route
`/lab` or `/sessions`

## File Location
`apps/web/src/app/lab/page.tsx`
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/SessionCard.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions listed with all info
- [ ] Sorting and filtering work
- [ ] Actions work correctly
- [ ] Delete has confirmation
- [ ] Pagination/infinite scroll works

 {"id":"brenner_bot-reew.5","title":"Implement Mobile-Responsive Session Experience","description":"# Implement Mobile-Responsive Session Experience

## Purpose
Ensure the full session experience works well on mobile devices.

## Mobile Challenges
1. Limited horizontal space
2. Touch-based interaction
3. Keyboard covers content
4. Multiple panels don't fit side-by-side

## Mobile Adaptations

### Phase Timeline
- Switch to vertical stepper
- Or compact horizontal with icons only
- Swipe to navigate phases

### Session Dashboard
- Stack panels vertically
- Hypothesis card as collapsible header
- Brenner quotes in expandable section

### Operator Sessions
- Full-width content area
- Step navigation via swipe or buttons
- Quote sidebar becomes bottom sheet

### Agent Tribunal
- Agents in accordion/tabs
- One agent visible at a time
- Expand to see full response

### Evidence Recording
- Full-screen modal
- Multi-step as swipeable pages

## Implementation Approach
- Use Tailwind responsive utilities
- Consider mobile-first CSS
- Test on real devices

## Touch Interactions
- Swipe to navigate
- Long-press for context menus
- Pull-to-refresh for agent updates

## File Location
Various components with responsive variants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All phases work on mobile
- [ ] No horizontal scrolling required
- [ ] Touch interactions feel native
- [ ] Text is readable
- [ ] Actions are tappable

 {"id":"brenner_bot-v2e","title":"Build native React document renderer with custom components","description":"# Task

Build a rich document renderer using NATIVE React/TypeScript components that fully leverage Tailwind and our design system. NOT a generic markdown viewer!

# Philosophy

The content deserves custom treatment:
- Brenner quotes should look *amazing* (not just gray italic blocks)
- Operator symbols (⊘, 𝓛, ≡, ✂) should be styled distinctively
- Code blocks should use our OKLCH colors
- Tables should be interactive, not HTML tables
- § anchors should be beautiful and functional

# Architecture

```
Markdown source → MDX/AST parser → Custom React components
```

1. Parse markdown at build time (or server-side)
2. Map each AST node to a custom component
3. Each component uses our design system

# Custom Components Needed

## Typography
- `<Heading level={1|2|3|4} id='anchor'>...</Heading>`
- `<Paragraph>...</Paragraph>`
- `<Lead>...</Lead>` (intro paragraphs)

## Brenner-specific
- `<BrennerQuote author='Brenner' source='§42'>...</BrennerQuote>` - Special quote styling with gradient border, attribution
- `<OperatorSymbol symbol='⊘' name='Level-split' />` - Styled operator with tooltip
- `<SectionAnchor id='§42' />` - Animated anchor with copy functionality

## Content blocks
- `<CodeBlock language='typescript'>...</CodeBlock>` - Syntax highlighting with our colors
- `<Table columns={[...]} data={[...]} />` - Interactive table with sorting, hover
- `<Callout type='insight|warning|note'>...</Callout>` - Styled callout boxes
- `<Definition term='Gedanken organism'>...</Definition>` - Glossary-style definitions

## Interactive
- `<CopyButton text='...' />` - Copy citation/text
- `<ShareButton quote='...' />` - Share to social/clipboard
- `<CollapsibleSection title='...'>...</CollapsibleSection>`

# Implementation

Use `next-mdx-remote` or `@mdx-js/mdx` to:
1. Parse markdown to AST
2. Provide custom components mapping
3. Render with our components

```tsx
import { MDXRemote } from 'next-mdx-remote/rsc';

const components = {
  h1: (props) => <Heading level={1} {...props} />,
  blockquote: (props) => <BrennerQuote {...props} />,
  // ...
};

<MDXRemote source={content} components={components} />
```

# Quote Styling Example

```tsx
function BrennerQuote({ children, source }) {
  return (
    <motion.blockquote
      className="relative my-8 border-l-4 border-primary/50 bg-card/50 p-6 
                 rounded-r-xl italic text-lg leading-relaxed"
      initial={{ opacity: 0, x: -20 }}
      whileInView={{ opacity: 1, x: 0 }}
    >
      <span className="absolute -left-3 -top-3 text-4xl text-primary/30">"</span>
      {children}
      {source && (
        <cite className="mt-4 block text-sm text-muted-foreground not-italic">
          — Sydney Brenner, {source}
        </cite>
      )}
    </motion.blockquote>
  );
}
```

# TOC Generation

Still generate TOC from headings, but render with our components:
- Sticky sidebar on desktop
- Collapsible on mobile  
- Scroll-spy highlighting
- Smooth scroll on click

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All markdown elements map to custom components
- [ ] Typography uses our design system
- [ ] Quotes look beautiful (not generic)
- [ ] Operators have distinctive styling
- [ ] § anchors are interactive
- [ ] TOC works with scroll-spy
- [ ] Animations respect reduced motion
- [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:14:46.390974262-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.1.1 + brenner_bot-5so.4.1.2","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:24.931287472-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.908825369-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-wpb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.941047723-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-v41w","title":"Task: Create Transition History Logging","description":"# Task: Create Transition History Logging

## Objective
Implement persistent logging of all state transitions for audit trail and history queries.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/hypothesis-history.ts`

### Storage Structure
```
.research/
└── transitions/
    ├── RS-20251230-transitions.json  # Per-session
    └── ...
```

### File Format
```json
{
  "sessionId": "RS-20251230",
  "transitions": [
    {
      "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
      "fromState": "proposed",
      "toState": "active",
      "trigger": "activate",
      "triggeredBy": "user",
      "timestamp": "2025-12-30T10:05:00Z"
    },
    {
      "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
      "fromState": "active",
      "toState": "killed",
      "trigger": "kill",
      "triggeredBy": "T-RS20251230-002",
      "reason": "ATAC-seq showed open chromatin, contradicting methylation-only hypothesis",
      "timestamp": "2025-12-31T15:30:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Core Functions
```typescript
export async function logTransition(transition: StateTransition): Promise<void>

export async function getHypothesisHistory(hypothesisId: string): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export async function getSessionTransitions(sessionId: string): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export async function getTransitionsInRange(
  start: Date,
  end: Date
): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export interface HypothesisTimeline {
  hypothesisId: string;
  statement: string;
  transitions: Array<{
    fromState: HypothesisState;
    toState: HypothesisState;
    trigger: TransitionTrigger;
    timestamp: string;
    triggeredBy?: string;
  }>;
  currentState: HypothesisState;
}

export async function buildHypothesisTimeline(
  hypothesisId: string
): Promise<HypothesisTimeline>
```

### Query Examples
```typescript
// "What killed hypothesis H1?"
const history = await getHypothesisHistory('H-RS20251230-001');
const killTransition = history.find(t => t.toState === 'killed');
// → T-RS20251230-002 killed it at 2025-12-31T15:30:00Z

// "What happened in session RS-20251230?"
const transitions = await getSessionTransitions('RS-20251230');
// → [activate H1, activate H2, kill H1, ...]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All transitions logged to per-session files
- [ ] Query by hypothesis ID
- [ ] Query by session ID
- [ ] Query by time range
- [ ] Timeline reconstruction shows full history
- [ ] Append-only log (no editing history)

## Dependencies
- Task: Define State Transition Types

## Effort Estimate
~150 lines of code, ~2 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:05.370418529-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T12:04:03.696867615-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T12:04:03.696867615-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in apps/web/src/lib/schemas/hypothesis-lifecycle.ts: transitionHypothesis function (line 292), TransitionHistoryStore class (line 469), and 72 passing unit tests.  {"id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","title":"EPIC: Testing Infrastructure & Full Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Goal
Achieve comprehensive test coverage using **real implementations** where possible.

# Mocking Philosophy (CLARIFIED)

**✅ DO test with real implementations:**
- Internal modules (parsers, state management, utilities)
- DOM rendering via happy-dom
- React hooks and components
- Data transformations and business logic

**✅ ACCEPTABLE to mock/stub:**
- `framer-motion` - Animation timing causes test flakiness
- External network calls in **unit tests** (not E2E) - for isolation
- Browser APIs not available in test env (IntersectionObserver, etc.)

**❌ DO NOT mock:**
- Internal module dependencies (import real modules)
- Component children (render real children)
- State management (use real stores)

# Current State (2025-12-30)
- **Unit tests**: 41 files, 50% coverage threshold
- **E2E tests**: 5 spec files
- **Logging**: Excellent infrastructure exists

# Critical Gaps
**Unit Tests Missing:**
- 16+ UI components (SpotlightSearch, DistillationViewer, SessionForm, nav, etc.)
- Library modules (corpus.ts, prompt-builder.ts, search/*)

**E2E Gaps:**
- Search workflow (MAIN USER JOURNEY - untested!)
- Session lifecycle, error handling, auth flows

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 80% line coverage (up from 50%)
- [ ] All interactive components tested with real DOM
- [ ] All critical user journeys have E2E coverage
- [ ] CI fails on coverage regression

# Structure
- **Unit Tests**: Real DOM, real state, real parsers
- **E2E Tests**: Real application, real data, detailed logging
- **Infrastructure**: Fixtures, coverage enforcement, accessibility","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:49:24.662121237-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T14:34:47.75397559-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T14:34:47.75397559-05:00","close_reason":"All children completed: Test Infrastructure Enhancements (4ep3), Unit Test Coverage (8dy3), E2E Integration Tests (hvi7). Coverage thresholds enforced, real implementations used, CI integration complete.","labels":["testing quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:35:55.792252655-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-voa","title":"TanStack Query: Migrate Document Viewers to Query Hooks","description":"# Goal
Migrate TranscriptViewer and DistillationViewer to use the new Query hooks instead of receiving data as props from Server Components.

## Background
Currently, document viewers receive pre-fetched data as props:
```typescript
// TranscriptViewer.tsx
interface TranscriptViewerProps {
  data: TranscriptData;  // Passed from Server Component
}
```

This works but means:
1. Data is re-fetched on every navigation
2. No caching between page visits
3. Server must parse on every request

## Migration Strategy

### Option A: Keep Server Fetch + Hydrate (Recommended)
Server Component prefetches, passes to client via HydrationBoundary.
- Best for SEO (content in initial HTML)
- Best for performance (no client-side loading flash)
- Query still provides caching for subsequent navigations

### Option B: Full Client-Side Fetch
Remove server prefetch, let Query handle everything.
- Simpler code
- Shows loading state on first visit
- Worse SEO

**We choose Option A** because the corpus pages are content-heavy and benefit from SSR.

## Implementation

### 1. Update corpus/[doc]/page.tsx
```typescript
import { HydrationBoundary, QueryClient, dehydrate } from '@tanstack/react-query';

export default async function CorpusDocPage({ params }) {
  const queryClient = new QueryClient();
  
  await queryClient.prefetchQuery({
    queryKey: ['corpus', 'doc', params.doc],
    queryFn: () => readCorpusDoc(params.doc),
  });

  return (
    <HydrationBoundary state={dehydrate(queryClient)}>
      <DocumentViewer docId={params.doc} />
    </HydrationBoundary>
  );
}
```

### 2. Update TranscriptViewer
```typescript
export function TranscriptViewer({ docId }: { docId: string }) {
  const { data, isLoading, error } = useCorpusDoc(docId);
  
  if (isLoading) return <TranscriptSkeleton />;
  if (error) return <ErrorBoundary error={error} />;
  
  // ... rest of component
}
```

### 3. Update DistillationViewer similarly

## Considerations

### Skeleton Components
Create loading skeletons that match the layout:
- TranscriptSkeleton: TOC + content placeholders
- DistillationSkeleton: Header + section placeholders

### Error Boundaries
Create error UI for:
- Document not found
- Parse failure
- Network error

### Backward Compatibility
During migration, support both patterns:
- Accept data as prop (old pattern)
- Use Query if docId provided (new pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TranscriptViewer uses Query hook
- [ ] DistillationViewer uses Query hook
- [ ] SSR still works (content in initial HTML)
- [ ] Navigation between docs is instant (cached)
- [ ] Loading states are graceful
- [ ] Error states are handled

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/src/app/corpus/[doc]/page.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptViewer.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationViewer.tsx

## Files to Create
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptSkeleton.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationSkeleton.tsx","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:54.657062985-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","close_reason":"Migrated document viewers to TanStack Query hooks with HydrationBoundary for SSR prefetching and navigation caching. Created DocumentContentClient.tsx client component that uses useCorpusDoc hook.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-voa","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1r5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:16:17.466921916-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
